mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, directly increasing S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and weakening consumer demand, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in >60% of instances when the unemployment rate crosses the specified threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates robust household balance sheets and consumer confidence, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces negative forward returns in >55% of occurrences or interaction term loses statistical significance in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production reflects strengthening manufacturing output and supply-side capacity utilization, driving positive short-term S&P 500 returns over the next week.
failure_condition: Signal direction reverses during high-inflation regimes or Rank IC falls below 0.01 over any 12-month rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Sharp monthly increases in the consumer price index trigger expectations of aggressive central bank tightening, compressing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Positive forward returns occur in >65% of high-inflation months or the signal loses predictive power when CPIAUCSL exceeds 3.0%.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in total nonfarm payrolls and a declining unemployment rate confirms broad-based labor market strength, supporting corporate earnings growth and positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The combined labor signal yields negative returns in >50% of cases or the interaction effect is statistically indistinguishable from zero in walk-forward validation.

mechanism_narrative: Higher quarterly real GDP growth reflects expanding economic activity and corporate revenue potential, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next week as markets price in improved fundamentals.
failure_condition: Forward returns are negative in >55% of high-GDP-growth quarters or the correlation between GDPC1 growth and 5-day returns falls below 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: Three consecutive months of declining industrial production signal a manufacturing contraction and weakening supply-side momentum, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The contraction signal fails to predict negative returns in >60% of instances or Rank IC remains below 0.02 across all tested rolling windows.
